semantic.role: CLI command adapter for the MCP (Model Context Protocol) subcommands: start, setup, and general command routing.
responsibilities: Start an MCP server by instantiating ArchSpineMCPServer and calling start()
Configure MCP client config files by invoking setupMcpConfigs and presenting results to the user
Route MCP subcommands based on CLI arguments, calling the appropriate handler with validated options
outOfScope: MCP server implementation details (handled by ArchSpineMCPServer in infra)
MCP configuration file generation logic (handled by setupMcpConfigs)
Any pipeline, persistence, or scanning logic
invariants: Must remain a thin CLI adapter: no business logic, no database access, no pipeline orchestration; all substantive work must be delegated to infra modules.
Must use throwCliUsage for invalid argument scenarios to maintain consistent CLI error presentation.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a thin CLI adapter that exposes MCP-related functionality (start, setup) to the command-line interface, strictly delegating to infra-layer modules.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Refactored MCP command handling to align with new CLI architecture, removing legacy alias support and simplifying subcommand routing.

publicSurface.description: Interface that defines the options object for the MCP command handler: args, rootDir, and config.
Starts an MCP server for the given root directory by creating an ArchSpineMCPServer instance and calling its start method.
Configures MCP client settings for the project by calling setupMcpConfigs and logging status to the user.
Dispatches the MCP subcommand (start, setup) based on the provided args array, or throws usage on invalid arguments.
